steps.title: Write price in terms of units sold
steps.content: Let $n$ be the number of products sold. The price starts at $90$ dollars when $0$ products are sold, then drops by $1.50$ for each additional product sold, so $$\text{price}=90-1.5n.$$

steps.title: Set up the revenue equation
steps.content: Revenue equals price times quantity: $$(90-1.5n)n=1336.50.$$ Expanding and rearranging gives $$90n-1.5n^2=1336.50,$$ $$1.5n^2-90n+1336.50=0.$$ Divide by $1.5$: $$n^2-60n+891=0.$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: Use the quadratic formula or factor by the roots. The discriminant is $$60^2-4(891)=3600-3564=36.$$ Thus $$n=\frac{60\pm 6}{2},$$ giving $n=33$ or $n=27$.

steps.title: Match the answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $27$: one valid solution, so this could be the number sold. ✓
  • Choice B — $30$: gives revenue $30(90-45)=1,350$, not $1,336.50$. ✗
  • Choice C — $831$: far exceeds the zero-price point at $n=60$, so it is impossible in this model. ✗
  • Choice D — $1,350$: also far exceeds the zero-price point. ✗
The other mathematical root, $33$, is not listed, so A is correct.
